<user_query>

</user_query>

Searching the codebase for failure-handler team leader and member prompts.

[REDACTED]

input.query: Where are failure handler team leader and member prompts defined?

content.text:
Checking how instructions are assembled and whether member agents have separate prompts.

[REDACTED]

The **Coder Failure Handler** does not use separate Langfuse prompts named “leader” and “member” like Project Team does. The layout is different.

## Team leader (coordinator / router)

The coordinating `Team` gets its policy from **Langfuse**, not from a file in the repo. Names are in `agents/coder_failure_handler/constants.py`:

| Role | Langfuse prompt name | Dict key |
|------|----------------------|----------|
| Router (team leader policy) | `coder-failure-handler/router` | `router` |
| Failure comment extraction | `coder-failure-handler/parse-failure-comment` | `parse_failure_comment` |
| Bug report template | `coder-failure-handler/bug-report` | `bug_report` |

Fetch wiring: `agents/coder_failure_handler/langfuse_prompts.py` → assembled in `agents/coder_failure_handler/build.py` via `_assemble_instructions()` → set on `Team.instructions` at build time.

```58:74:agents/coder_failure_handler/build.py
def _assemble_instructions(prompts: dict[str, str]) -> str:
    router = prompts[ROUTER_PROMPT_KEY].rstrip()
    parse_contract = prompts[PARSE_FAILURE_COMMENT_PROMPT_KEY].strip()
    bug_report_template = prompts[BUG_REPORT_PROMPT_KEY].strip()

    parts = [
        router,
        f"\n\n## Failure Comment Extraction Contract\n\n{parse_contract}",
        f"\n\n## Bug Report Template\n\n{bug_report_template}",
        ...
    ]
    return "".join(parts)
```

The **actual prompt text** for those three lives in your **Langfuse** project (label `production` by default). It is not checked into this repo; issue-145 notes they must be authored there.

Startup: `app/main.py` calls `build_coder_failure_handler_team(...)`, which fetches Langfuse unless you pass a `prompts=` dict (as unit tests do).

## Members (no failure-handler Langfuse prompts)

Members are three **existing** agents, each with **inline** instructions in code — not Langfuse under `coder-failure-handler/*`:

| Member | Build | Instructions location |
|--------|--------|------------------------|
| GitHub | `agents/github_agent.py` | `_INSTRUCTIONS` (~lines 32–37) |
| AgentOS debug | `agents/debug_agent.py` | `DEBUG_AGENT_INSTRUCTIONS` (~lines 55–68) |
| Coder run debug | `agents/coder/run_debug.py` | `_INSTRUCTIONS_TEMPLATE` + embedded `workflows/coder/workflow.yaml` |

They are wired in `build_coder_failure_handler_team()` as `members=[github_agent, debug_agent, coder_run_debug_agent]`; only the **Team** gets the Langfuse-assembled router instructions.

---

**Contrast:** Project Team *does* have `project-team/leader` (and steward/discovery prompts) in `agents/project_team/constants.py` and `agents/project_team/langfuse_prompts.py`. Failure handler uses `coder-failure-handler/router` for the coordinator and does not add per-member Langfuse prompts.

If you meant Project Team’s leader/member prompts instead, say so and we can map those names too.

[REDACTED]

content.text:
**No — the GitHub agent is not new.** What landed in the last ~24 hours is the **Coder Failure Handler team**, which **reuses** the existing `build_github_agent` factory.

## Timeline

| What | When |
|------|------|
| `agents/github_agent.py` (`github-agent`) | Added **2026-05-20** (`feat: migrate GitHub MCP to hosted official server`) |
| Recent touch | **2026-05-28** — sleep toolkit on that agent |
| Failure handler team | **2026-05-29** — issue #145 / PR #147; wires a **second** `build_github_agent(db)` instance into the team |

So the module and agent id predate the failure handler by over a week.

## Where it’s exposed (not registry-only)

The GitHub agent is a **top-level AgentOS agent**, not something introduced only for the failure handler:

```407:426:app/main.py
    agents=[
        concierge_agent,
        mcp_agent,
        *_github_agents(),
        synapse_agent,
        ...
    ],
    teams=[_project_team, _coder_failure_handler_team],
```

`_github_agents()` returns `build_github_agent(db)` plus the repo explorer. Studio quick prompts already reference `github-agent` in `app/config.yaml`.

The **Registry** (`Registry(tools=_registry_tools(), ...)`) exposes **tools** (shared GitHub MCP via `platform_deps.mcp`, shell, workflow runners, etc.). It does **not** register the GitHub agent as a registry component — agents live on the `AgentOS(agents=[...])` list.

## Failure handler vs standalone

For the failure handler, `main.py` builds a **dedicated** instance so team sessions don’t share history with the standalone agent:

```172:177:app/main.py
_coder_failure_handler_github_agent = _build_github_agent(db)
_coder_failure_handler_team = build_coder_failure_handler_team(
    db,
    github_agent=_coder_failure_handler_github_agent,
```

Same factory, same `id="github-agent"`, **different object** — only on the team’s `members` list, not a third entry in `agents=[...]`.

**Project Team** does not use `build_github_agent` at all; stewards/leader get GitHub via `build_mcp_tools(...)` toolkits, not this agent.

**Summary:** Existing, publicly listed `github-agent`; failure handler adds a second instance as a team member only. The new piece in the last day is the team + Langfuse router prompts, not the GitHub agent itself.

[REDACTED]

def build_coder_failure_handler_team(
    db: Any,
    *,
    github_agent: Agent,
    debug_agent: Agent,
    coder_run_debug_agent: Agent,
    settings: CoderFailureHandlerSettings | None = None,
    prompts: dict[str, str] | None = None,
) -> Team:
    """Build the Coder Failure Handler team.

    Parameters
    ----------
    db:
        Agno PostgresDb for session/run persistence.
    github_agent:
        Pre-built GitHub agent (existing ``build_github_agent`` instance).
    debug_agent:
        Pre-built AgentOS debug agent (existing ``build_debug_agent`` instance).
    coder_run_debug_agent:
        Pre-built coder run debug agent (existing ``build_coder_run_debug_agent`` instance).
    settings:
        Langfuse credentials; loaded from env when ``None``.
    prompts:
        Pre-fetched prompt bodies (key → text); fetched from Langfuse when ``None``.
        Keys: ``"router"``, ``"parse_failure_comment"``, ``"bug_report"``.
    """
input.new_string:

    prompts: dict[str, str] | None = None,
) -> Team:
    """Build the Coder Failure Handler team.

    Parameters
    ----------
    db:
        Agno PostgresDb for session/run persistence.
    leader_github_tools:
        GitHub MCP toolkit for the coordinating team leader (issues, comments, labels).
    debug_agent:
        Pre-built AgentOS debug agent (existing ``build_debug_agent`` instance).
    coder_run_debug_agent:
        Pre-built coder run debug agent (existing ``build_coder_run_debug_agent`` instance).

    """

input.contents:
"""Unit tests for build_coder_failure_handler_team."""

from __future__ import annotations

from unittest.mock import patch

import pytest
from agno.team.mode import TeamMode

from agents.coder.run_debug import (
    CODER_RUN_DEBUG_AGENT_ID,
    build_coder_run_debug_agent,
)
from agents.coder_failure_handler.build import (
    CoderFailureHandlerSettings,
    build_coder_failure_handler_team,
)
from agents.coder_failure_handler.constants import (
    CODER_FAILURE_HANDLER_GITHUB_TOOLS_NAME,
    CODER_FAILURE_HANDLER_TEAM_ID,
    LEADER_GITHUB_TOOLS,
)
from agents.debug_agent import DEBUG_AGENT_ID, build_debug_agent
from agents.github_agent import github_mcp_tools

_PROMPTS = {
    "router": "You are the failure handler router.",
    "parse_failure_comment": "Extract run_id from this comment.",
    "bug_report": "File a bug with title {{title}}.",
}

_SETTINGS = CoderFailureHandlerSettings(
    langfuse_public_key="pk",
    langfuse_secret_key="sk",
    langfuse_base_url="https://langfuse.example",
)


def _set_env(monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch) -> None:
    monkeypatch.setenv("GITHUB_TOKEN", "********")
    monkeypatch.setenv("LANGFUSE_PUBLIC_KEY", "pk")
    monkeypatch.setenv("LANGFUSE_SECRET_KEY", "sk")
    monkeypatch.setenv("LANGFUSE_BASE_URL", "https://langfuse.example")


def _leader_github_tools():
    return github_mcp_tools(
        include_tools=list(LEADER_GITHUB_TOOLS),
        name=CODER_FAILURE_HANDLER_GITHUB_TOOLS_NAME,
    )


def _build(monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch, **overrides: object) -> object:
    _set_env(monkeypatch)
    db = object()
    dbg = build_debug_agent(db)
    crd = build_coder_run_debug_agent(db)
    kwargs: dict[str, object] = {
        "db": db,
        "leader_github_tools": _leader_github_tools(),
        "debug_agent": dbg,
        "coder_run_debug_agent": crd,
        "settings": _SETTINGS,
        "prompts": _PROMPTS,
    }
    kwargs.update(overrides)
    return build_coder_failure_handler_team(**kwargs)  # type: ignore[arg-type]


def test_team_structure(monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch) -> None:
    team = _build(monkeypatch)

    assert team.id == CODER_FAILURE_HANDLER_TEAM_ID
    assert team.mode == TeamMode.coordinate
    assert len(team.members) == 2
    assert isinstance(team.instructions, str)
    assert "router" in team.instructions.lower() or "failure" in team.instructions.lower()
    assert "rebaseai-coder-failed" in team.instructions
    assert "rebaseai-coder-failure-report" in team.instructions

    team_tools = team.tools or []
    assert len(team_tools) == 1
    assert team_tools[0].name == CODER_FAILURE_HANDLER_GITHUB_TOOLS_NAME


def test_instructions_embed_all_three_prompts(monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch) -> None:
    team = _build(monkeypatch)

    assert "You are the failure handler router." in team.instructions
    assert "Extract run_id from this comment." in team.instructions
    assert "File a bug with title" in team.instructions


def test_member_order(monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch) -> None:
    _set_env(monkeypatch)
    db = object()
    dbg = build_debug_agent(db)
    crd = build_coder_run_debug_agent(db)

    team = build_coder_failure_handler_team(
        db,
        leader_github_tools=_leader_github_tools(),
        debug_agent=dbg,
        coder_run_debug_agent=crd,
        settings=_SETTINGS,
        prompts=_PROMPTS,
    )

    assert team.members[0].id == DEBUG_AGENT_ID
    assert team.members[1].id == CODER_RUN_DEBUG_AGENT_ID


def test_langfuse_fetch_called_when_prompts_none(monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch) -> None:
    _set_env(monkeypatch)
    db = object()
    dbg = build_debug_agent(db)
    crd = build_coder_run_debug_agent(db)

    with patch(
        "agents.coder_failure_handler.build.fetch_coder_failure_handler_prompts_for_credentials",
        return_value=_PROMPTS,
    ) as mock_fetch:
        team = build_coder_failure_handler_team(
            db,
            leader_github_tools=_leader_github_tools(),
            debug_agent=dbg,
            coder_run_debug_agent=crd,
            settings=_SETTINGS,
        )

    mock_fetch.assert_called_once_with(
        public_key="pk",
        secret_key="sk",
        base_url="https://langfuse.example",
    )
    assert _PROMPTS["router"] in team.instructions
    assert _PROMPTS["parse_failure_comment"] in team.instructions
    assert _PROMPTS["bug_report"] in team.instructions


def test_langfuse_failure_propagates(monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch) -> None:
    _set_env(monkeypatch)
    db = object()
    dbg = build_debug_agent(db)
    crd = build_coder_run_debug_agent(db)

    with patch(
        "agents.coder_failure_handler.build.fetch_coder_failure_handler_prompts_for_credentials",
        side_effect=ValueError("prompt missing"),
    ):
        with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="prompt missing"):
            build_coder_failure_handler_team(
                db,
                leader_github_tools=_leader_github_tools(),
                debug_agent=dbg,
                coder_run_debug_agent=crd,
                settings=_SETTINGS,
            )

input.description: Re-run ruff and mypy after import fix

content.text:
Refactored the **Coder Failure Handler** to match the Project Team pattern: the coordinating leader gets GitHub MCP tools directly; the GitHub agent is no longer a team member.

## Changes

**`agents/coder_failure_handler/constants.py`** — Added `LEADER_GITHUB_TOOLS` (`get_me`, `list_issues`, `issue_read`, `issue_write`, `add_issue_comment`, `search_issues`) and a toolkit name for the filtered MCP connection.

**`agents/coder_failure_handler/build.py`**
- Replaced `github_agent` with `leader_github_tools: Toolkit`
- `members` is now `[debug_agent, coder_run_debug_agent]` (2 members)
- `tools=[leader_github_tools]` on the `Team`

**`app/main.py`**
- Removed the dedicated `_coder_failure_handler_github_agent` instance
- Builds `_coder_failure_handler_github_tools` via `build_mcp_tools(..., include_tools=LEADER_GITHUB_TOOLS)`
- Standalone `github-agent` in `agents=[...]` is unchanged

**Tests** — Updated for 2 members, leader tools on `team.tools`, and new build signature.

## Members and GitHub

Only the former GitHub **member** needed GitHub access. **AgentOS debug** and **coder-run debug** stay as members with their own tools (Postgres / filesystem). No GitHub tools were added to them.

If you already persisted `coder-failure-handler-team` in Postgres with three members, Studio may still show the old snapshot until that component row is removed or the team is re-saved. New boots use the 2-member + leader-tools shape.

All 7 unit tests in `tests/unit/coder_failure_handler/` pass.

[REDACTED]
